summary: Smoking Scarlet>Fire jam and tight Playin'→Built transition fuel a strong show
blurb: The second set delivers a scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain jam and an inspired Playing In The Band → Built To Last segue. Brent’s keyboards and Phil’s bass lock in for deep jams, while Jerry’s vocals show occasional strain. Overall a solid late‑80s performance with several standout moments.
review: April 6, 1989 at Crisler Arena finds the Dead riding high on classic pairings and tight interplay. The first set rolls through a bright Touch of Grey and a buoyant Althea before landing on a soaring Bird Song that sets the tone for what’s to come. In the second set the band ignites with a smoking Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain jam, then launches into an inspired Playing In The Band → Built To Last transition that showcases Brent Mydland’s haunting keys and Phil Lesh’s bass‑forward groove. Though Jerry Garcia’s voice shows occasional strain and his guitar can dip in the mix, his lead work remains compelling, especially during the marathon The Other One and the concluding Wharf Rat/Around & Around medley. Drummers Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann provide a nuanced, prodding pulse that drives each segue forward. With strong moments across both sets and an energetic encore of Brokedown Palace, this show stands out as a solid example of the band’s late‑80s chemistry.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of nine recordings are clearly positive, repeatedly praising the Scarlet>Fire jam, the Playin'→Built transition, and Brent & Phil’s tight jams. No consistent negative remarks appear; mixed reviews only note minor mix quirks. This strong, consistent praise justifies a high positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crisp clarity, captures the full band balance and highlights the standout Scarlet>Fire jam and Playin'→Built transition.

key_highlights: Smoking Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain jam that drives the second set.
Inspired Playing In The Band → Built To Last segue with tight jams and vocal energy.
Brent Mydland’s keyboard textures intertwine beautifully with Phil Lesh’s bass throughout.

band_performance.Jerry: Vocals show occasional strain and a slight dip in power later in the set, but lead guitar work remains compelling when audible.
band_performance.Phil: Bass sits forward and rich in the mix, delivering strong grooves that lock in with Brent’s keyboards during jams.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid rhythm foundation; his guitar can sit a touch high in the mix on some recordings but overall contributes reliably.
band_performance.Brent: In top form, delivering haunting ballads and driving jam textures; his interplay with Phil is a highlight of the night.
band_performance.Mickey: Offers nuanced, prodding percussion that adds depth to transitions and drives the flow of the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are solid and supportive, propelling segues like Playin' → Built without any major complaints.
